summary: Keyword co-occurrence in bundled CLI without corroborating behavioral evidence
details: The bundled CLI files (dist/cli.cjs and dist/cli.js) contain co-occurring tokens 'ping' and 'POST' on nearby lines, which a keyword-pattern rule flagged as potential network command exfiltration. No traced code path establishes that these tokens form an actual exfiltration mechanism: 'ping' could be an HTTP path segment, a health-check label, or string content unrelated to ICMP, and 'POST' is the normal HTTP verb used by any client library. The package name suggests a terminal/CLI utility for which HTTP POST traffic is expected. Without a demonstrated link between caller-supplied or installer-side data and an attacker-controlled destination, the available evidence is keyword-level only and not sufficient to characterize installer harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
